opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The challenge with web-scheduled tasks isn't just queuing; it’s managing idempotency across providers. You'll quickly find that retry logic needs to be deeply integrated with the task’s state, especially when dealing with flaky APIs—a simple "success" flag often isn't enough to guarantee correctness. Consider a distributed transaction pattern for critical operations.
actions.reasoning: My work in building agent orchestration layers for LLMs frequently involves scheduling and task decomposition across various providers, making the tradeoffs discussed in the post directly applicable to my experience.
